texts.full_text: Narayana Menon (7 October 1887– 31 October1954) wasaMalayalam language author fromKerala state,SouthIndia. His oeuvre consists of poems, plays and translations. His best known works include Paavangal, a translation of VictorHugo's Les Misérables, andthe elegy kannuneerthulli. Early life Hewas born in Punnayurkulam, Ponnani taluk in South Malabar on 7 October 1887.Heobtained English education fromKunnamkulam,Trichur andCalicut. While hewas 18, he met poet Vallathol and became hischief companion. Literary careerVallathol'sinfluenceis obvious inhis early poems. He was also influencedby Robert G. Ingersoll and was an atheist for most ofhis youth.Later he developed strong interest inIndian philosophy and religion and was influenced bythe ideas of Dayananda Saraswati and SwamiVivekananda. Most of hisearly prose works wereon subjects related to Indian philosophy.His most famous poem Kannuneerthulli(Tear Drop)was an elegy written afterthe death of hiswife. Paavangal,a translation of Victor Hugo's Les Misérables, was a milestone inthe historyof Malayalam literature and it set off asocial reformation of sorts in Kerala. Aquatic habitat of mosquito possesses a wide range of physicochemical factors that may strongly influence the abundance of different mosquito involving disease transmission. Many environmental factors may have effect on larval abundance and the transmission of mosquito-borne diseases. These factors include temperature, humidity, rainfall, and the presence of standing water, which can serve as breeding sites. Understanding the influence of these environmental factors on mosquito larval habitats can help in developing effective control and prevention strategies. Temperature and humidity, in particular, can significantly affect the development and survival of mosquito larvae. Warmer temperatures can accelerate the development of larvae, leading to a higher mosquito population in a shorter period. Conversely, extreme temperatures, either too high or too low, can be detrimental to larvae survival. Humidity also plays a crucial role, as it can affect the evaporation of water in breeding sites, thereby influencing the availability of suitable habitats for mosquito larvae. texts.full_text: why there is confusion about her voice type. The distinction between a soprano and a mezzo-soprano involves more than just range; it also includes timbre, vocal weight, and where the voice is most comfortable and has the most power and resonance. Here are some key factors that help differentiate between a soprano and a mezzo-soprano: 1. Vocal Range: As you mentioned, sopranos typically have a higher range, extending up to C6 or even higher, while mezzo-sopranos usually go up to A5. However, the range alone does not define the voice type. 2. Timbre: Sopranos tend to have a lighter, brighter timbre, while mezzo-sopranos have a darker, richer, and denser sound. This is one of the main differences you'll notice when listening to these voice types. 3. Vocal Weight: Mezzo-sopranos typically have a heavier vocal weight, which means their voices have more depth and warmth, while sopranos have a lighter vocal weight. 4. Tessitura: This refers to the range where a singer's voice is most comfortable and has the most power and resonance. A soprano's tessitura is higher than a mezzo-soprano's. Even if a mezzo-soprano can sing the same high notes as a soprano, the soprano will generally have an easier time singing them with more power and resonance. 5. Passaggio: This is the area where a singer's voice transitions from one register to another. Sopranos and mezzo-sopranos have different passaggio areas, which can help determine their voice type. In the case of Maria Callas, her repertoire and the roles she sang throughout her career are what cause the confusion about her voice type. She had a unique voice that allowed her to sing both soprano and mezzo-soprano roles, but her timbre, vocal weight, and tessitura were more characteristic of a soprano. texts.full_text: a 2nd installation of a different version (from iso), what would I do differently? Yes, extra SFS files are add-ons, such as the latest 5-Mariner-5C_490.sfs. These add-ons must reside in the base of the partition, as Jim1911's example in /mnt/sda2/, if that is the partition you're installing Lighthouse to. /mnt is where partitions are typically mounted at, so /mnt/sda2 or /mnt/home are still the base of sda2. It is a bit confusing, as one might assume that only '/' is the base, but when a partition is mounted it's base becomes /mnt/<partition name>. The other confusing part is that the main file spup-490.sfs for Lighthouse or lupu-501.sfs for Lucid Puppy is also an SFS, however it is not an 'extra SFS' but rather the main file that is used to boot, and so spup-490.sfs must stay with the other boot files, initrd.gz, vmlinuz. Again these boot files must stay together and can either be in the base of the partition (if you only have one puplet on that partition), or in a folder one-level-deep (allows sharing the partition with other puplets). When you save your session to a pupsave that file which contains all of the updated and personal files you have saved during each session will also reside in the same folder as the boot files discussed here. Recently, attempts have been made to produce such thermoelectric devices using thin film thermoelectric materials. Thermoelectric devices generate power by having a temperature difference between a hot junction and a cold junction. One method of improving the efficiency therefore is to reduce the heat conducted from the hot junction to the cold junction as much as possible and to create a larger temperature difference between the two junctions. However, in conventional thermoelectric devices, the amount of heat released from the thermoelectric material surface is not sufficiently affected. texts.full_text: multifunctional, serine protease enzyme and receptor with roles in the control of endocrine and immune function, cell metabolism, growth and adhesion. As an enzyme, DPP IV cleaves the N-terminal dipeptide from the incretin hormones glucagon-like peptide-1 and glucose-dependent insulinotropic polypeptide. This inactivates the hormones, thereby cancelling their prandial insulinotropic effect. One approach to restore incretin activity as a therapy for Type 2 diabetes has been the development of DPP IV inhibitors. Inhibitors of DPP IV are currently used clinically not only because they improve glycemic control by reducing postprandial hyperglycemia but also reduce body weight and do so with good tolerability profiles compared to other antidiabetic drugs such as sulfonylureas or insulin. The error you are encountering is due to the fact that the `Where` method requires a `Func` delegate that matches a specific signature. In your case, the `Func` delegate should take an `item` of the type `T` (where `T` is the type of the elements in the `itemsToShow` collection) and return a `bool` indicating whether the `item` should be included in the filtered collection. The `Func` delegate is defined as `Func<T, bool>`, where `T` is the type of the input parameter and `bool` is the return type. The `Where` method signature in the `System.Linq` namespace is as follows: ```csharp System.Linq.Enumerable.Where<TSource>( System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable<TSource> source, System.Func<TSource, bool> predicate ) ``` Where: - `TSource` is the type of the elements of `source` - `source` is the `IEnumerable` collection you want to filter - `predicate` is the `Func` delegate that defines the filter condition In your code, `item => item.Visible` is the lambda expression that defines the `predicate` delegate. texts.full_text: whom I have a long-standing relationship – so I guess you can say I come out of traditional publishing. But I’m also an entrepreneur, and I’ve always loved the idea of my books being my own. It’s just taken me longer to get there. Now that I am there – I’m on fire with the possibilities. As for marketing mistakes – I don’t know, as my first novel has yet to come out. Check with me in about a year. TR: I’ll be happy to. So, now that you mentioned it, tell us a bit about your first book. VC: My first novel, The Bone Church, is coming out this fall. It’s a Cold War spy thriller with a ghostly twist and I have never loved writing anything more. Hopefully, my readers will feel that intense emotion. It involves a mysterious Roman Catholic Cardinal, a reckless sculptor intent on making a big political statement, a gypsy with a dangerous sex life, and an émigré priest who is forced to confront the fragility of his faith. There’s love and sex and danger all over the place. TR: It sounds fascinating! And your second one? VC: My second book is written. It’s called The Hungarian and I’m publishing it late next Spring. texts.full_text: Trip Reports – Other Advertiser Disclosure With So Many Airport Shops Still Closed, One Man Has Taken It On Himself To Sell You Snacks by Gary Leff on September 6, 2021 Comedian Steven Wright once said that he was kicked out of a movie theater for bringing his own food. His argument, he said, was that concession stand prices are outrageous. And besides, he hadn’t had a barbecue in a long time. Airport concessions can be high-priced. But the bigger challenge is that so many of them remain closed as concessionaires struggle to find employees (and the airport works hard to keep wages down). That means fewer places to buy snacks and long lines for the places that are open. One man has stepped into the breach,...by selling his own food from a portable cart in one of Los Angeles International Airport's terminals. His goal is not only to fill an important consumer gap but also to demonstrate how much money airports could be making if they were able to attract more concessionaires and keep wages high. Dr. Gendler specializes in digestive diseases and pancreatic/biliary endoscopy (ERCP) and has performed over 17,000 endoscopies in his 25 year career. Dr. Gendler’s office will be located at 1296 North Avenue in New Rochelle. For appointments and more information on his practice, call (914) 235-9333. #### White Plains Hospital (WPH) is a 292-bed voluntary, not-for-profit health care organization with the primary mission of offering high quality, acute health care and preventive medical care to all people who live in, work in or visit Westchester County and its surrounding areas. Centers of Excellence include the Dickstein Cancer Treatment Center, The William & Sylvia Silberstein Neonatal & Maternity Center and The Ruth and Jerome A. Siegel Stroke Center. The Hospital has the busiest Emergency Department in Westchester County, seeing nearly 55,000 visits a year. White Plains Hospital is the only community hospital in Westchester County licensed to perform emergency & elective angioplasty. The Hospital is a ten-time winner of the Consumer Choice Award, an honor given to the nation’s top hospitals by the National Research Corporation, and received Magnet designation in 2012 from the American Nurses Credentialing Center. WPH is a member of the New York-Presbyterian Healthcare System and the Stellaris Health Network, Inc. For additional information, visit http://www.wphospital.org. texts.full_text: This study evaluates the association of vitamin D and calcium, from food and/or supplements, to breast density in premenopausal and postmenopausal women separately. A total of 777 premenopausal and 783 post-menopausal women recruited at two radiology clinics in Quebec City, Canada, in 2001 to 2002, completed a food frequency questionnaire to assess vitamin D and calcium. Breast density from screening mammograms was assessed using a computer-assisted method. Associations between vitamin D or and breast density were evaluated using linear regression models. Adjusted means in breast density were assessed according to the combined daily intakes of the two nutrients using generalized linear models. In premenopausal women, total intakes of vitamin D and calcium were inversely related to breast density (beta = -1.4; P = 0.004 for vitamin D; beta = -0.8; P = 0.0004 for calcium). In multivariate linear regression, simultaneous increments in daily total intakes of 400 IU vitamin D and 1,000 mg calcium were associated with an 8.5% (95% confidIn premenopausal women, the results indicated that higher intake levels of both nutrients could potentially lead to a decrease in breast density which may suggest lower risk for dense tissue- one key factor suggesting hereditary background for developing types of cancer. On comparing with post-menopausal woman cohort where vitamin D and calcium intakes did not show any significant association (P>0.05), this highlights the potential impact that these nutrients have on breast density during premenstrual cycle phase when hormone levels may influence its effects more dramatically. This study, though correlational in nature does point towards an important link between diet factors such as vitamin D and calcium intake from food or supplements to mammographic measures of breast tissue composition. These results could provide further direction

They have this unusual conversation that is very natural and makes you wonder what would happen if all conversations around love were so normal. The characters played by Mithra Senthil Kumar, a trans man with non-binary gender identity (he/him) from Chennai ,and Vignesh Prasanna who plays the role of an Indian gay male character are two young men exploring their sexuality and identities in this short film. On the other hand, natural selection in wild and especially weedy relatives presumably favours trait values that increase the probability of escaping eradication. Gene flow between crops and wild species may also counter human-mediated selection, promoting the evolution and persistence of weedy forms. In this issue, two studies from a group of collaborators examine diversity and divergence patterns of genes underlying two traits associated with red rice (Oryza sp.), the conspecific relative of cultivated rice (Oryza sativa) that is a non-native weed (see Fig. 1). In the first study by Gross et al. (2010), genetic variation in the major gene underlying the hallmark red pigmentation characterizing most weedy rice (Rc) is found to have a pattern consistent with non-reversion from U.S. cultivated rice (i.e. the cultivar did not 'go feral').
